codeReview.summary: MockLlm implementation is functionally correct and all 5 tests pass. The feature was already fully implemented in the codebase prior to this work session. Implementation provides predictable responses via MockLlm::new(), custom response support via MockLlm::with_response(), and failure simulation via MockLlm::failing(). Global call count tracking works via get_call_count() and reset_call_count().

issues.description: The `call_count` field in MockLlm struct has #[allow(dead_code)] but is never used - only the global static CALL_COUNT is used for tracking. The instance field is redundant dead code.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The handoff references commit f6ed1d2, but that commit actually implements swell-tools MCP client, not llm-mock-backend. The mock backend was already implemented in the codebase. This mismatch between handoff commitId and actual implementation commit was not documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git show f6ed1d2 shows changes to crates/swell-tools/src/mcp.rs only. mock.rs has no changes in this commit.

summary: Feature implementation passes review. MockLlm provides predictable responses, custom response support, and failure simulation as required. All 5 tests pass and workspace builds successfully. Minor issue: the commit referenced in handoff does not correspond to this feature's implementation.
